552.reason: [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but fails to handle cases where '&' or '|' appear inside string literals correctly. However, more critically, it does NOT skip over string literals — it stops entirely when encountering a '"', meaning any '&' or '|' AFTER a string in the same line will be ignored. But the real bug is that this loop breaks on '"' and never resumes scanning after the closing quote, so if an illegal & or | appears after a string, it won't be detected. However, the immediate critical flaw for error type 'a' is that the lexical analyzer (getNextToken) already handles tokens outside strings, and this function is meant to scan the whole line for stray &/|, but it aborts at the first unescaped quote regardless of context.]
